guyz
why is 9-12 called ant?
because of the size comparison?
also
why are we called keepers?
is it because each keeper... keeps something?
alistair keeps the black pool, and us the door?
(ik the full title is keeper of secrets but...)
ant is....hard to explain, unfortunately it's a meaning lost in translation
(or was it secret keeper? i forgor)
(but everybody be forgetting everything thanks to dissolution, doesnt seem a big thing to keep...)
this chinese idiom is used in ch9 as part of the stage flavor info
ohhh omg
conceptually it's quite similar to the western story of david vs goliath, except david wins whereas this idiom is more used to describe something futile/pointless
the idea being that you are going up against something *so much greater* than you that you have not the faintest chance at victory
that is very thematic for 9-12
yep, it's essentially used to convey how futile your struggles are
and thus the climactic final fight of ch9 is "ant", because you are the ant challenging a tree
...but yeah, not really translatable unfortunately, so it's something that has quite the significance in chinese but is basically meaningless in english
it's possible that some people gain the correct subtext of your existence being as meaningless as an ant, but it's not really that clear and not as obvious of a meaning as the original
I struggled with that stage title for pretty much an entire night before giving up as there's just not really any elegant way to preserve it without significantly changing the name of the stage
"A mere ant compared to a god"
yeah but I can't use that as a stage title!!!

kinda reminds me of a tumblr post that describe the horror of recognizing the eldritch as "an ant on a computer circuit board suddenly fully understanding how it works and not knowing what to do with the info"
i can totally see it, the stage details text do try to explain it, but it isnt as clear too x.x
i floated the idea of maybe changing it to be something like "Insignificant" but it kind of just doesn't quite hit it
how about "Mere Ant(s)"
yeah the stage details was a direct nod to the idiom but it made no sense in english so I at least tweaked the details a bit to explain it somewhat
but the stage name...
this one is better than just ant
i felt like using an adjective in the name would kind of lack that sort of like, finality
the single-hit impact of like, just a single noun and nothing else
"speck" might be a more accurate english word but that makes for a terrible name
ohoh, and what about our title? keeper of secrets. Do you guys have an idea why are we called that?
i feel like this is common enough in the 'eldritch horror' sphere that 'ant' actually works pretty well
like it's not the same meaning but
'you are to a god as an ant is to you' is intuitive and works
maybe its because keepers know more about dissolution compared to investigators but they cant tell anyone beneath them about it regardless
I forgor, is every investigator capable of seeing the dissolution?
this probably wouldn't work but off the top of my head, the closest western idiom I can think of would be king canute and the tide
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/King_Canute_and_the_tide

embeds.title: King Canute and the tide
embeds.description:
The story of King Canute and the tide is an apocryphal anecdote meant to illustrate the piety or humility of King Canute the Great (also written as Cnut), recorded in the 12th century by Henry of Huntingdon.
In the story, Canute demonstrates to his flattering courtiers that he has no control over th
